steps.title: Find the first line intercepts
steps.content: For $x+5y=5$, set one variable equal to $0$ at a time. When $y=0$, the equation becomes $x=5$. When $x=0$, the equation becomes $5y=5$, so $y=1$. This line passes through $(5,0)$ and $(0,1)$.

steps.title: Find the second line intercepts
steps.content: For $2x-y=-4$, use the same intercept check. When $y=0$, $2x=-4$, so $x=-2$. When $x=0$, $-y=-4$, so $y=4$. This line passes through $(-2,0)$ and $(0,4)$.

steps.title: Choose the matching graph
steps.content: Choose the graph with one line through $(5,0)$ and $(0,1)$ and the other line through $(-2,0)$ and $(0,4)$. The answer is C.
